assertions.title: Mixed env/config values drive fallback retrieval in one execution

steps.action: Write only unpaywall_email to temp HOME global config
steps.expected: Required fallback credential comes from global config
steps.observed: Global config contained only unpaywall_email: fallback@example.com

steps.action: Run direct get --as pdf --fallback with deterministic fallback endpoint overrides only in process env
steps.expected: One execution merges config credential with env endpoint overrides
steps.observed: Fallback command exited 0 with winning_stage=unpaywall and ordered attempts across env-selected endpoints

evidence.network: GET /openaire/search/researchProducts -> 200, GET /openaire/search/publications -> 200, GET /core -> 200, GET /europepmc -> 200, GET /pmc/esearch.fcgi -> 200, GET /unpaywall/10.1000%2Fruntime-fallback-1?email=fallback@example.com -> 200, GET /files/unpaywall.pdf -> 200

assertions.title: Direct CLI commands remain usable without wrapper-era required-env preflight

steps.action: Run search-paper-cli version with temp HOME and no global config
steps.expected: Version succeeds without wrapper-era required-env preflight
steps.observed: Exit 0 with version text on stdout and empty stderr

steps.action: Run search-paper-cli sources with same empty temp HOME
steps.observed: Exit 0 with valid JSON stdout, empty stderr, and gated acm/ieee sources left disabled

assertions.title: Standalone release artifact works outside the repository with global config

steps.action: Copy built artifact to an outside-repo bin directory under the assigned evidence boundary
steps.expected: Standalone binary runs without repository-relative runtime dependencies

steps.action: Run outside-repo sources, search, get --as pdf, and get --as text with temp HOME global config
steps.expected: Artifact honors the same global-config model outside the repository
steps.observed: All outside-repo commands exited 0 and retrieval flows wrote files under the outside-repo cwd

assertions.title: Outside-repo direct invocation merges env and global config in one run

steps.action: Set SEARCH_PAPER_ACM_API_KEY in process env only and ieee_api_key in global config only for one outside-repo run
steps.expected: One sources --format json payload reports both acm and ieee enabled
steps.observed: sources payload reported acm.enabled=true and ieee.enabled=true together

assertions.title: The same merged config is visible to sources, search, and preferred get retrieval flows

steps.action: Run outside-repo sources with env ACM + config IEEE using one temp HOME/cwd
steps.expected: Merged config is visible in sources output
steps.observed: sources showed both acm and ieee enabled

steps.action: Run outside-repo search using the same HOME/env and config-provided arxiv_base_url override
steps.expected: search uses the same effective config
steps.observed: search returned one paper from the mocked arxiv endpoint

steps.action: Run outside-repo get --as pdf and get --as text using the searched paper JSON under the same HOME/env
steps.expected: Preferred retrieval flows stay usable without wrapper mediation
steps.observed: PDF download succeeded and text extraction succeeded with machine-readable stdout and empty stderr

assertions.title: Direct fallback retrieval works without wrapper-managed setup

steps.action: Run outside-repo direct get --as pdf --fallback with global-config unpaywall_email and env deterministic overrides
steps.expected: CLI preserves JSON stdout and stderr-only diagnostics while fallback succeeds
steps.observed: Exit 0 with JSON stdout, empty stderr, winning_stage=unpaywall, ordered attempts, and a saved PDF file

evidence.network: GET /openaire/search/researchProducts -> 200, GET /openaire/search/publications -> 200, GET /core -> 200, GET /europepmc -> 200, GET /pmc/esearch.fcgi -> 200, GET /unpaywall/10.1000%2Fruntime-fallback-1?email=fallback@example.com -> 200, GET /files/unpaywall.pdf -> 200

summary: Tested 6 assigned direct-CLI runtime assertions. All 6 passed using direct CLI invocations plus a standalone copied artifact in hermetic temp HOME/outside-repo contexts.
